## Test Coverage / DX & Tooling / Docs / Direction
(Reviewer B — read-only, 2026-07-03. Baseline re-used from above: type-check,
lint, 491 tests all clean.)

### Coverage map (critical paths)
- WELL covered: subscription sync server side (app-factory.test.js — ETag,
  If-Match/412, tombstones, stale push, malformed If-Match),
  channel-search public surface (channel-search.test.js — identity detect,
  query building, dedupe/rank, Feedy HTML discovery + API fallback),
  feed-refresh-policy (19KB test), feed-fetcher, sqlite-store/backup,
  migrations-runner, security-middleware.
- GAPS below.

### Findings (leverage-ordered)

#### [TEST-01] Cover the feed-aggregator orchestrator before the Feedy refactor lands
- Evidence: `server/feed-aggregator.js` — the highest-churn file (60 commits)
  and the feature the repo exists for. `runAggregation` (~lines 200–380:
  redirect merge, temp-subscription resolution, quota reset, batch loop,
  archive merge, cache writes), `aggregateFeeds` (overlap coalescing),
  `aggregateOnStartupIfStale` (stale-cache logic), `startScheduledRefresh`
  (timer lifecycle) have NO tests. The only test,
  `server/feed-aggregator-internals.test.js`, has 2 tests (refreshBatch
  metadata, setRunningAggregationStatus). The `__test__` export
  (feed-aggregator.js ~480–490) does NOT even expose `runAggregation` /
  `aggregateFeeds`, so the core path is unreachable from tests by design.
- Cross-ref: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-03-feedy-youtube-refresh-discovery-design.md`
  "Aggregator tests cover" explicitly lists bounded concurrency, overlap
  coalescing, startup/scheduled/manual trigger parity, partial-failure,
  incremental status, archive/watched/queue/favorite/Shorts preservation —
  none present.
- Impact: every change to the refresh engine (the in-progress Feedy rewrite)
  lands with no safety net; regressions in quota reset, redirect merge, or
  archive eviction are invisible until production.
- Effort: M (expose runAggregation via __test__ with injectable appStore/
  fetchers; characterization tests for the 6 spec-listed behaviors).
- Risk: MED — refactor to make the singleton injectable could surface
  coupling, but no public API changes.
- Confidence: HIGH (read the code + test + spec).
- Fix sketch: thread a deps object (appStore, feedFetcher, clock) into
  createFeedAggregator and export runAggregation under __test__; add tests
  for stale-cache skip, 412-style overlap join, and partial-batch failure.

#### [TEST-02] Client-side sync reconciliation & persistence are untested
- Evidence: `src/lib/server-sync.ts` (200 LOC: `applyServerRedirects`/tombstones
  line 51, `pushToServer` 412 handling line 163, `forcePushToServer` 412 retry
  line 197), `src/lib/sync-reconcile.ts` (282 LOC), `src/lib/indexeddb.ts`
  (692 LOC — largest lib file, the client persistence layer) have ZERO tests.
  Asymmetry: the SERVER half of this exact path is integration-tested in
  app-factory.test.js, but the CLIENT conflict/merge/tombstone logic is not.
- Impact: optimistic-concurrency conflict handling and tombstone deletion
  reconciliation — the data-integrity boundary for multi-browser use — regress
  silently.
- Effort: M. Risk: LOW (pure functions, easy to unit test). Confidence: HIGH.
- Fix sketch: unit-test mergeSubscriptionLists, applyServerRedirects (tombstone
  + redirect), and the 412 → revision-recorder flow with mocked fetch.

#### [TEST-03] shorts-status.js — no tests despite being central to the feed path
- Evidence: no `server/shorts-status.test.js`; module is imported and called
  throughout feed-aggregator.js (enrichVideosWithShortsStatus,
  applyLocalShortsMetadata, backfill). Fns are pure/DI-ready:
  `looksLikeShortByLocalMetadata` (regex heuristics),
  `isArchivedShortsBackfillDue(now=Date.now())`, `resolveYouTubeShortsStatus(
  ..., httpClient=axios)`.
- Impact: the Shorts filter (a headline feature) depends on regex + duration
  heuristics with no characterization; a regex tweak silently re-classifies
  the whole archive.
- Effort: S. Risk: LOW. Confidence: HIGH.
- Fix sketch: unit tests for the text/thumbnail/duration heuristics and the
  backfill-due clock logic; mock httpClient for resolveYouTubeShortsStatus.

#### [TEST-04] /api/resolve-channel public route is untested
- Evidence: `server/app-factory.js:490–532` — documented public API (also
  named in the Feedy spec's API-compatibility contracts). `grep -rl
  "resolve-channel" server/` returns only the route file itself; no test
  references it. It carries real input validation (type/value, 256-char cap,
  regex `^[\w.@\-/]+$`, handle vs custom_url branching, 503/404 mapping).
- Impact: the single-channel "add by handle/URL" path's validation and error
  mapping can regress with no signal.
- Effort: S. Risk: LOW. Confidence: HIGH.
- Fix sketch: supertest cases in app-factory.test.js for missing/invalid
  input, handle & custom_url success (mock axios), 404 no-channelId, 503
  disabled.

#### [DX-01] .env.example omits the documented feed-refresh env vars
- Evidence: README "Configuration" documents `FEED_REFRESH_ENABLED`,
  `FEED_REFRESH_ON_START`, `FEED_REFRESH_INTERVAL_MINUTES`; docker-compose.yml
  sets all three. `.env.example` lists API rate-limit + origins + auth vars but
  NONE of the feed-refresh trio.
- Impact: new operators configuring background refresh must read README/compose,
  not the example file they're told to copy.
- Effort: S. Risk: LOW. Confidence: HIGH.
- Fix sketch: add the three feed-refresh vars (commented, with defaults) to
  `.env.example`.

#### [DX-02] No type checking on the JavaScript server
- Evidence: `server/package.json` has no `type-check`/`lint` script; server is
  plain CommonJS JS. Root `eslint.config.js` DOES lint `server/**/*.js`
  (no-unused-vars, no-dupe-keys) and CI runs `npm run lint`, so lint is
  covered — but type errors in sqlite-store/channel-search/feed-aggregator JS
  are invisible. `tsc --allowJs --checkJs` (or JSDoc typing) is absent.
- Impact: shape/type drift between sqlite-store JSON blobs and their consumers
  won't surface at build time.
- Effort: M (add JSDoc types + a checkJs tsconfig; non-trivial on a mature JS
  server). Risk: MED (initial pass likely flags many issues to triage).
- Confidence: HIGH that it's absent; MED that it's worth doing now (deliberate
  JS choice; integration tests mitigate). Reported as an option, not a must.
- Fix sketch: opt-in `tsc --noEmit --allowJs --checkJs` with `// @ts-nocheck`
  escape hatches only where JSDoc'ing is disproportionate.

#### [DOC-01] Project AGENTS.md has drifted from the renamed codebase
- Evidence: `AGENTS.md:17` container image
  `ghcr.io/greg-hass/youtube-subscriptions:latest` vs actual
  `ghcr.io/greg-hass/mytube:latest` (docker-compose.yml). `AGENTS.md` env
  table lists `FEED_REFRESH_INTERVAL_MINUTES` default **15** vs code default
  **5** (`feed-refresh-policy.js:2` `DEFAULT_SCHEDULED_REFRESH_INTERVAL_MS
  = 5*60*1000`; compose sets 5; README says 5). AGENTS.md Architecture Notes
  points DB at `server/data/youtube-subscriptions.sqlite` vs primary
  `server/data/mytube.sqlite` (`app-store.js:8–9`; the youtube-subscriptions
  file is now the migrated-legacy path). AGENTS.md also omits
  `FEED_REFRESH_ON_START`.
- Impact: AGENTS.md is the binding process doc agents follow — a wrong image
  name and wrong DB path in it directly mislead agent-driven deploys/edits.
- Effort: S. Risk: LOW. Confidence: HIGH.
- Fix sketch: update the three values (image, interval, primary DB path) and
  add FEED_REFRESH_ON_START.

#### [DIR-01] Finish or formally descope the multi-resolver removal the Feedy spec mandates
- Evidence (direction, adapted format):
  - `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-03-feedy-youtube-refresh-discovery-design.md`
    "Removal scope" directs removing Brave, LLM, OpenCode, Piped, and
    Invidious from discovery/refresh. But `server/channel-search.js` STILL
    wires them in: `resolveChannelViaLlm` (import line 14, used line 614),
    `PIPED_INSTANCES` (line 368), `INVIDIOUS_INSTANCES` (line 414),
    `parseYouTubeUrl` from brave-channel-search (line 7/647).
    `llm-channel-resolver.js` (26 KB) and `brave-channel-search.js` remain.
    `opencode-channel-resolver.js` is an 8-line back-compat shim carrying a
    26 KB mis-named test (`opencode-channel-resolver.test.js`).
  - Blast radius (also un-touched): `nginx.conf` CSP connect-src still lists
    ~12 piped/invidious origins; `.env.example` still documents
    `BRAVE_API_KEY` + `OPENCODE_API_KEY`; `external-services.json` still
    ships piped/invidious instance lists.
  - Cross-ref: sibling findings DEBT-01 (dead Piped/Invidious code in
    channel-search) and DEBT-02 (opencode shim + deprecated aliases) are the
    code-level face of this same half-finished decision.
- Impact (product value): completing the removal delivers the spec's stated
  payoff — operational clarity and one RSS/HTML/API path instead of five
  flaky third-party tiers (piped/invidious instances rot constantly). It also
  removes the 26 KB LLM resolver and its key-handling surface.
- Confidence: HIGH that the removal is intended-but-undelivered (spec dated
  today, actively in progress); the call to finish vs keep a tier is the
  maintainer's.
- Effort: L (multi-file removal + CSP/.env/spec/.env.example cleanup +
  re-pointing the mis-named test). Risk: MED (must keep YouTube API fallback
  + public API contracts intact; spec lists them).
- Fix sketch: scope as a design/finish plan — delete the four modules + their
  channel-search call sites, strip piped/invidious from CSP +
  external-services.json + .env.example, rename/inline the opencode test, and
  confirm Feedy discovery + API fallback remain green.

  dedupe/rank, Feedy HTML discovery + API fallback), feed-refresh-policy
  (19 KB test), feed-fetcher, sqlite-store/backup, migrations-runner,
  security-middleware.
- GAPS below.

### Findings (leverage-ordered)

#### [TEST-01] Cover the feed-aggregator orchestrator before the Feedy refactor lands
- Evidence: `server/feed-aggregator.js` — highest-churn file (60 commits) and
  the feature the repo exists for. `runAggregation` (~lines 200–380: redirect
  merge, temp-subscription resolution, quota reset, batch loop, archive merge,
  cache writes), `aggregateFeeds` (overlap coalescing),
  `aggregateOnStartupIfStale` (stale-cache logic), `startScheduledRefresh`
  (timer lifecycle) have NO tests. The only test,
  `server/feed-aggregator-internals.test.js`, has 2 tests (refreshBatch
  metadata, setRunningAggregationStatus). The `__test__` export
  (feed-aggregator.js ~480–490) does NOT even expose `runAggregation`/
  `aggregateFeeds`, so the core path is unreachable from tests by design.
- Cross-ref: `docs/.../2026-07-03-feedy-youtube-refresh-discovery-design.md`
  "Aggregator tests cover" lists bounded concurrency, overlap coalescing,
  startup/scheduled/manual trigger parity, partial-failure, incremental
  status, archive/watched/queue/favorite/Shorts preservation — none present.
- Impact: every change to the refresh engine (the in-progress Feedy rewrite)
  lands with no safety net; regressions in quota reset, redirect merge, or
  archive eviction are invisible until production.
- Effort: M. Risk: MED (making the singleton injectable could surface
  coupling; no public API change). Confidence: HIGH.
- Fix sketch: thread a deps object (appStore, feedFetcher, clock) into
  createFeedAggregator, export runAggregation under __test__, add tests for
  stale-cache skip, overlap join, and partial-batch failure.

#### [TEST-02] Client-side sync reconciliation & persistence are untested
- Evidence: `src/lib/server-sync.ts` (200 LOC: `applyServerRedirects`/
  tombstones line 51, `pushToServer` 412 handling line 163,
  `forcePushToServer` 412 retry line 197), `src/lib/sync-reconcile.ts`
  (282 LOC), `src/lib/indexeddb.ts` (692 LOC — largest lib file, the client
  persistence layer) have ZERO tests. Asymmetry: the SERVER half of this
  exact path is integration-tested in app-factory.test.js, but the CLIENT
  conflict/merge/tombstone logic is not.
- Impact: optimistic-concurrency conflict handling and tombstone deletion
  reconciliation — the data-integrity boundary for multi-browser use —
  regress silently.
- Effort: M. Risk: LOW (pure functions). Confidence: HIGH.
- Fix sketch: unit-test mergeSubscriptionLists, applyServerRedirects
  (tombstone + redirect), and the 412 → revision-recorder flow with
  mocked fetch.

#### [TEST-03] shorts-status.js — no tests despite being central to the feed path
- Evidence: no `server/shorts-status.test.js`; module is imported/called
  throughout feed-aggregator.js (enrichVideosWithShortsStatus,
  applyLocalShortsMetadata, backfill). Fns are pure/DI-ready:
  `looksLikeShortByLocalMetadata` (regex heuristics),
  `isArchivedShortsBackfillDue(now=Date.now())`, `resolveYouTubeShortsStatus(
  ..., httpClient=axios)`.
- Impact: the Shorts filter (a headline feature) depends on regex + duration
  heuristics with no characterization; a regex tweak silently re-classifies
  the whole archive.
- Effort: S. Risk: LOW. Confidence: HIGH.
- Fix sketch: unit tests for text/thumbnail/duration heuristics + backfill-due
  clock logic; mock httpClient for resolveYouTubeShortsStatus.

#### [TEST-04] /api/resolve-channel public route is untested
- Evidence: `server/app-factory.js:490–532` — documented public API (also in
  the Feedy spec's API-compatibility contracts). `grep -rl "resolve-channel"
  server/` returns only the route file; no test references it. Carries real
  validation (type/value, 256-char cap, regex `^[\w.@\-/]+$`, handle vs
  custom_url branching, 503/404 mapping).
- Impact: the single-channel "add by handle/URL" path's validation and error
  mapping can regress with no signal.
- Effort: S. Risk: LOW. Confidence: HIGH.
- Fix sketch: supertest cases for missing/invalid input, handle & custom_url
  success (mock axios), 404 no-channelId, 503 disabled.

#### [DX-01] .env.example omits the documented feed-refresh env vars
- Evidence: README "Configuration" documents `FEED_REFRESH_ENABLED`,
  `FEED_REFRESH_ON_START`, `FEED_REFRESH_INTERVAL_MINUTES`; docker-compose.yml
  sets all three. `.env.example` lists API rate-limit + origins + auth vars
  but NONE of the feed-refresh trio.
- Impact: new operators configuring background refresh must read README/compose,
  not the example file they're told to copy.
- Effort: S. Risk: LOW. Confidence: HIGH.
- Fix sketch: add the three feed-refresh vars (commented, with defaults) to
  `.env.example`.

#### [DX-02] No type checking on the JavaScript server
- Evidence: `server/package.json` has no `type-check`/`lint` script; server is
  plain CommonJS JS. Root `eslint.config.js` DOES lint `server/**/*.js`
  (no-unused-vars, no-dupe-keys) and CI runs `npm run lint`, so lint is
  covered — but type errors in sqlite-store/channel-search/feed-aggregator JS
  are invisible. `tsc --allowJs --checkJs` (or JSDoc typing) is absent.
- Impact: shape/type drift between sqlite-store JSON blobs and their consumers
  won't surface at build time.
- Effort: M (JSDoc types + a checkJs tsconfig; non-trivial on a mature JS
  server). Risk: MED (initial pass likely flags many issues to triage).
- Confidence: HIGH that it's absent; MED that it's worth doing now
  (deliberate JS choice; integration tests mitigate). Reported as an option.
- Fix sketch: opt-in `tsc --noEmit --allowJs --checkJs` with `@ts-nocheck`
  escape hatches only where JSDoc'ing is disproportionate.

#### [DOC-01] Project AGENTS.md has drifted from the renamed codebase
- Evidence: `AGENTS.md:17` container image
  `ghcr.io/greg-hass/youtube-subscriptions:latest` vs actual
  `ghcr.io/greg-hass/mytube:latest` (docker-compose.yml). AGENTS.md env table
  lists `FEED_REFRESH_INTERVAL_MINUTES` default **15** vs code default **5**
  (`feed-refresh-policy.js:2` `DEFAULT_SCHEDULED_REFRESH_INTERVAL_MS =
  5*60*1000`; compose sets 5; README says 5). AGENTS.md Architecture Notes
  points DB at `server/data/youtube-subscriptions.sqlite` vs primary
  `server/data/mytube.sqlite` (`app-store.js:8–9`; the youtube-subscriptions
  file is now the migrated-legacy path). AGENTS.md also omits
  `FEED_REFRESH_ON_START`.
- Impact: AGENTS.md is the binding process doc agents follow — a wrong image
  name and wrong DB path in it directly mislead agent-driven deploys/edits.
- Effort: S. Risk: LOW. Confidence: HIGH.
- Fix sketch: update the three values (image, interval, primary DB path) and
  add FEED_REFRESH_ON_START.

#### [DIR-01] Finish or formally descope the multi-resolver removal the Feedy spec mandates
- Evidence (direction, adapted format):
  - `docs/.../2026-07-03-feedy-youtube-refresh-discovery-design.md` "Removal
    scope" directs removing Brave, LLM, OpenCode, Piped, and Invidious from
    discovery/refresh. But `server/channel-search.js` STILL wires them in:
    `resolveChannelViaLlm` (import line 14, used line 614), `PIPED_INSTANCES`
    (line 368), `INVIDIOUS_INSTANCES` (line 414), `parseYouTubeUrl` from
    brave-channel-search (line 7/647). `llm-channel-resolver.js` (26 KB) and
    `brave-channel-search.js` remain. `opencode-channel-resolver.js` is an
    8-line back-compat shim carrying a 26 KB mis-named test
    (`opencode-channel-resolver.test.js`).
  - Blast radius also untouched: `nginx.conf` CSP connect-src still lists
    ~12 piped/invidious origins; `.env.example` still documents
    `BRAVE_API_KEY` + `OPENCODE_API_KEY`; `external-services.json` still
    ships piped/invidious instance lists.
  - Cross-ref: sibling findings DEBT-01 (dead Piped/Invidious code in
    channel-search) and DEBT-02 (opencode shim + deprecated aliases) are the
    code-level face of this same half-finished decision.
- Impact (product value): completing the removal delivers the spec's stated
  payoff — operational clarity and ONE RSS/HTML/API path instead of five
  flaky third-party tiers (piped/invidious instances rot constantly); also
  removes the 26 KB LLM resolver and its key-handling surface.
- Confidence: HIGH that the removal is intended-but-undelivered (spec dated
  today, actively in progress); finishing vs keeping a tier is the
  maintainer's call.
- Effort: L (multi-file removal + CSP/.env/external-services cleanup +
  re-pointing the mis-named test). Risk: MED (must keep YouTube API fallback
  + public API contracts intact; spec lists them).
- Fix sketch: scope as a design/finish plan — delete the four modules + their
  channel-search call sites, strip piped/invidious from CSP +
  external-services.json + .env.example, rename/inline the opencode test,
  confirm Feedy discovery + API fallback remain green.
